claim_status: Sandbox readiness-review template only; no owner review, no security review, no platform review, no quality review, no release-operations review, no packaged expected-deny probes, no effective platform policy review, no host-safe fixture review, no broker fixture review, no compromised-client harness review, no platform matrix review, no result-record review, no failure-denominator review, no cleanup review, no sandbox-readiness claim, no renderer-security claim, no site-isolation claim, no hostile-browsing safety claim, no platform containment claim, no SEC-GATE-1 claim, no SEC-GATE-6 claim, no production-safety claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real sandbox readiness review or name placeholder probe inventories, probe packages, owner reviewers, security reviewers, platform reviewers, quality reviewers, release-operations reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, or implied PB-012 readiness authority.

probe_package_axes.required_evidence: Owner-reviewed packaged role runner launches each role through the same process, handle set, sandbox primitive, and broker policy used by the evaluated configuration beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template.
Probe package records artifact build ID, source revision, command line, compiler profile, policy artifact hashes, stdout hash, and stderr hash.
Role launch path proves renderer, network, storage, GPU, decoder, extension, DevTools, agent, and updater roles run under their intended policy.
Inherited handles, brokered handles, descriptors, and platform-specific capabilities are captured before probes run.
Process identity, token, entitlement, namespace, package SID, integrity level, and role metadata are retained in result records.
Stable result records include role, platform, surface, attempted operation, expected result, actual result, exit code, artifact hashes, cleanup status, owner review, and failure denominator.
Every failed, skipped, unsupported, timed-out, cancelled, or rerun probe remains in the denominator before owner review.

platform_policy_axes.required_evidence: Windows evidence captures AppContainer or token identity, integrity level, restricted token, job object limits, process mitigations, dynamic-code policy, registry/COM/RPC/device/file/socket/debug denial logs, and inherited-handle inspection.

macOS evidence captures seatbelt or App Sandbox profile, entitlements, hardened runtime, JIT entitlement state, code signing identity, notarization or local test-signing state, broker descriptors, and sandbox extensions.

Linux evidence captures PID, mount, user, network, IPC, and cgroup namespace policy, seccomp filters, no-new-privileges, Landlock or portal policy, capabilities, cgroups, rlimits, broker descriptors, and denial logs.
Probe artifacts include effective platform policy, token/entitlement/namespace state, handle descriptors, degraded-state markers, unsupported-state markers, and policy artifact hashes.
macOS, Windows, and Linux evidence names OS version, primitive set, packaging mode, degraded state, unsupported state, and equivalent probe result coverage.
Unsupported, skipped, degraded, disabled, unavailable, or host-incompatible sandbox primitives are recorded as blockers or waivers, not passes.

role_surface_axes.required_evidence: Renderer expected-deny probes cover hostile web content with assigned IPC, shared memory, surfaces, and read-only font handles only.
Network expected-deny probes cover ambient sockets outside kernel-issued request context plus forbidden password-vault and arbitrary-profile authority.
Storage expected-deny probes cover cross-profile and private-session data outside assigned profile, origin, and partition handles.
GPU expected-deny probes cover unassigned surfaces, stale device generations, unvalidated shared memory, profile storage, network, credentials, and arbitrary filesystem access.
Decoder expected-deny probes cover hostile codecs, fonts, images, PDFs, archives, task-scoped input, bounded output, and no ambient authority.
Extension expected-deny probes cover ungranted host access, native messaging, incognito policy, update signing, and agent confirmation authority.
DevTools expected-deny probes cover hidden remote listener creation, wrong target, wrong epoch, unattached profile access, and release signing authority.
Agent expected-deny probes cover forged grants, stale document epoch, direct action execution, raw cookies, password vault, ungranted files, ungranted origins, and confirmation bypass.
Updater expected-deny probes cover wrong-target update commands, unsigned metadata, forged version roots, web profile content, browser history, agent data, signing keys, and arbitrary execution.
File probes deny arbitrary filesystem read/write, path traversal, symlink or junction escape, and cross-profile file access outside broker-issued task handles.
Socket probes deny ambient TCP, UDP, DNS, proxy bypass, loopback listener, and unauthorized destination access.
Process probes deny child process creation, shell execution, fork/exec, job escape, service launch, and inherited-handle abuse.
Registry probes deny Windows registry and platform configuration reads or writes outside brokered policy.
Device probes deny camera, microphone, display capture, USB, Bluetooth, serial, HID, graphics-device, raw input, and raw device access unless broker-issued and user-approved.
Shared-memory probes deny unbounded allocation, executable mapping, writable trusted metadata, stale generation reuse, and unbrokered cross-process mappings.
Credential probes deny keychain, password vault, cookie store, passkey, client certificate, payment identity, signing key, and provider-token access.
Debug probes deny debugger attach, ptrace, process memory read, remote debug listener creation, debug-port binding, crash dump expansion, and release-symbol access.
Profile probes deny profile directory access, cross-profile object access, private-session data, history/bookmark/cookie databases, and origin-store escape.
IPC probes deny unauthorized service connection, wrong-role message, forged identity, stale epoch, duplicate capability, platform IPC, COM/RPC/Mach/dbus escape, and internal protocol access.

host_safety_axes.required_evidence: Probe execution uses host-safe temporary roots, fake credentials, fake profiles, fake stores, loopback fixtures, and cleanup checks beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template.
Temporary roots are created under reviewed paths, bounded by size and lifetime, and cleaned or retained with hashes according to policy.
Credential probes use fake keychains, fake vaults, fake tokens, fake cookies, fake passkeys, and no real secrets.
Profile probes use fake profiles and real-profile-free fixtures with cleanup evidence and no production profile access.
Network probes use loopback-only fixtures unless owner-reviewed policy permits isolated external access.
Cleanup checks prove temporary roots, fake stores, processes, sockets, handles, profiles, and artifacts are removed or intentionally retained.

broker_compromised_axes.required_evidence: Brokered allowed operations have task-scoped fixtures so expected-deny probes distinguish ambient authority from narrowly authorized behavior.
Compromised-client harnesses forge IDs, origins, epochs, handles, paths, sizes, ordering, retries, disconnected state, and unauthorized broker calls.
Forged process, role, principal, profile, origin, site, document, extension, DevTools, agent, and update IDs are denied.
Stale document epochs and stale handles are denied across renderer, DevTools, agent, storage, and IPC paths.
Oversized paths, payloads, buffers, shared-memory requests, probe logs, and queues are bounded and denied safely.
Duplicate, reordered, retried, cancelled, timed-out, and disconnected client behavior cannot convert denied authority into a pass.
Broker exceptions are named, task-scoped, logged, reviewed, and cannot be counted as ambient-denial passes.
Probe logs, errors, crash dumps, traces, and audit records redact secrets and preserve enough context for owner review.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Owner accepts probe inventory, probe package, role/surface matrix, platform policy, host-safety fixtures, broker fixtures, compromised-client harnesses, result records, failure denominator, cleanup, and PB-012 status change beyond the checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template.
Security reviewer accepts threat-model coverage, compromised-client probes, broker-denial behavior, secret redaction, SEC-GATE implications, and residual risks.
Platform reviewer accepts Windows, macOS, and Linux effective policies, degraded modes, unsupported modes, and platform-specific evidence transfer limits.
Quality reviewer accepts failure denominator, retry policy, skipped/unsupported classification, cleanup evidence, and validation output.
Release-operations reviewer accepts packaged-probe build identity, artifact retention, reproducibility, and production-safety implications.
SEC-GATE-1 remains unsupported until owner-reviewed compromised-renderer containment probes pass with effective platform policy evidence.
SEC-GATE-6 remains unsupported until owner-reviewed release-path sandbox and hostile-browsing probes pass with effective policy and failure-denominator evidence.

PB-012 readiness promotion remains separate from sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing safety, platform containment, production safety, implementation, and Chrome-class approval.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim sandbox readiness-review template is cited as owner-reviewed sandbox readiness, PB-012 readiness, sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing safety, platform containment, SEC-GATE, production-safety, or implementation evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject PB-012 readiness, security, SEC-GATE, production, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Probe inventory, probe package, owner reviewer, security reviewer, platform reviewer, quality reviewer, or release-operations reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and sandbox readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Packaged expected-deny probes do not run through the same role runner, process, handle set, sandbox primitive, broker policy, and package mode as the evaluated configuration.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, SEC-GATE, and production-safety claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Effective platform policy, process identity, token/entitlement/namespace state, handle descriptors, degraded-state markers, unsupported-state markers, or policy artifact hashes are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject platform containment and sandbox claims.

rejection_rules.condition: The platform matrix for macOS, Windows, or Linux evidence is missing, transferred across platforms without review, or counted as equivalent despite unsupported or degraded primitives.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject platform-wide sandbox and production-safety claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Host-safe temporary roots, fake credentials, fake profiles, fake stores, loopback fixtures, cleanup checks, or bounded execution controls are absent.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject probe execution and abort before host mutation.

rejection_rules.condition: Broker fixtures are missing, so probes cannot distinguish ambient authority from narrowly authorized task-scoped operations.
Compromised-client harnesses do not forge IDs, origins, epochs, handles, paths, sizes, ordering, retries, disconnected state, and unauthorized broker calls.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject SEC-GATE and hostile-browsing safety claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Hidden failures, skipped probes, unsupported cases, degraded modes, timeouts, cancellations, retries, cleanup failures, or swallowed errors are omitted from the failure denominator.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Probes touch real secrets, real credential stores, real signing keys, real profile data, real provider tokens, or a production profile.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the run, rotate affected secrets if needed, and keep PB-012 partial.

rejection_rules.condition: Probes can mutate the host outside reviewed temporary roots, fake stores, broker fixtures, or cleanup controls, or can grow unbounded logs, queues, sockets, files, memory, or processes.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject probe execution and sandbox evidence.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing validation or template checks is cited as sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing safety, SEC-GATE, platform-containment, production-safety, implementation, release, or Chrome-class evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject security, production, release, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, no-claim wording, claim boundary wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement owner-reviewed sandbox evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-012 partial.
